task_completion.description: Open the specified network file in netedit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The netedit application is open and the network file 'acosta_buslanes.net.xml' is loaded (visible in the title bar or the road network is displayed in the viewport).

task_completion.description: Select the Traffic Light mode/tool in netedit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Traffic Light tool icon (usually a traffic light symbol) is selected or highlighted in the netedit toolbar.
A junction in the network is clicked and highlighted, and traffic light properties or phases appear in the left-hand panel.

task_completion.description: Change the duration of the first phase (row 0) to 45 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the traffic light program editor or properties table, the 'duration' field for the very first phase (index/row 0) is explicitly changed to '45'.

task_completion.description: Save the modified traffic light program or network.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or action is visible showing the agent saving the traffic light program to '/home/ga/SUMO_Output/modified_tls.xml' OR saving the modified network file.

integrity.description: The agent used the netedit GUI to make the changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Modifications must be done through the netedit graphical interface. The agent should not use a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) or terminal commands (like sed) to directly edit the XML files.

integrity.description: The agent actually interacted with the traffic light properties.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show intermediate steps of selecting the junction and editing the table, rather than just a final saved file appearing.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the tool by copy-pasting XML.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent copying a pre-written XML block and pasting it into a file to bypass using netedit's built-in phase editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target network file is 'acosta_buslanes.net.xml'. The target phase duration for the first phase (row 0) is exactly 45 seconds. While the prompt instructs the agent to save the traffic light program to 'modified_tls.xml', saving the original network file with the updated duration is also considered acceptable for completion.
